indicator: Public hospitals with strengthened oxygen supply systems (headline goal)
latest_verified_actual: No public national achievement percentage found; a separate World Bank project reported 80% of its designated hospitals had functional oxygen-delivery equipment
how_to_read_it: The World Bank result has a project-only denominator and cannot score the national 60% target. Best lead: the WHO-supported NHSP 2022–2026 midterm review was conducted and its M&E framework scores this exact indicator (Obj 25.7), but the report is not public.

latest_verified_actual: No national count published; shortages affecting operations reported at 2 Lusaka hospitals (contested)
how_to_read_it: No public actuals exist for the plan's most patient-relevant target. The parliamentary report is a warning signal, not a count. Absence of data is not evidence the target was missed.

indicator: Hospitals with oxygen production and refilling capacity
latest_verified_actual: At least 22 recent commissioned systems with stated cylinder-refilling capability: 15 World Bank mobile plants + Kitwe + 6 Global Fund–UNOPS plants; some were replacements or additions at existing plant sites
verdict: Installation progress likely reached the numeric target; functional total unconfirmed
how_to_read_it: The public project count reaches 22 recent systems and older plants also exist, but this is not a reconciled national count of currently functional hospitals. Do not sum programmes into a functionality claim without MOH confirmation.

indicator: Facilities with bulk liquid oxygen (LOX) tanks
latest_verified_actual: At least 2 public installations identified: Levy Mwanawasa (23,000 L / later called 20-ton) and Livingstone UTH (reported 20-ton); no national inventory
verdict: At least 2 of 15 publicly identified; no inventory published
how_to_read_it: Movement from zero is confirmed. Livingstone is supported by named technical officials but lacks a durable MOH press release; only MOH can confirm the current national count and operational status.

indicator: Oxygen manifolds installed at Level 1–3 hospitals
latest_verified_actual: 67–68 of 91 sampled facilities had a manifold or filling station (different definition); new plants at Kitwe, Kabwe, and St. Paul's include manifolds
how_to_read_it: The BMES counted facilities with any manifold/filling station in a sample; the plan counts installed units nationally. The definitions cannot be joined.

latest_verified_actual: No national procurement total published; ~1 functional device per 10 beds in the 91-hospital sample
how_to_read_it: No procurement series exists in public sources; the only measured value is sampled functional density, which is a different indicator.

indicator: Biomedical staff employed/trained for oxygen services
latest_verified_actual: No national count published; 42 of 91 sampled facilities had no available biomedical engineer/technician; Skills Award curriculum active at 2 colleges
verdict: Not publicly reported; sampled evidence shows gaps
how_to_read_it: The verified workforce actions (76 trainers, 672 nurses trained, 2 colleges) are clinical or educational; the plan's biomedical employment total has no published actual.

verdict: Not publicly reported; plan internally conflicts
how_to_read_it: The plan states both 1,200 and 3,614 as workforce end-states in different tables. This internal conflict is preserved, not resolved.
A priority confirmation item in the brief; no testing or indexing data has been published.

latest_verified_actual: No public result; dashboard designed to capture purity but only 3 of 11 surveyed respondents used it
how_to_read_it: Purity compliance cannot be assessed from public sources.

latest_verified_actual: 3 of 11 surveyed respondents reported any dashboard use (Aug 2022); Jan 2025 assessment found registers, SOPs, and data staff still missing
verdict: Not publicly reported; sampled evidence shows low use
how_to_read_it: Two independent measurements both show weak routine data capture, though neither is a national facility count.

latest_verified_actual: 2 installations publicly identified: Levy Mwanawasa and Livingstone UTH
verdict: 2 of 3 publicly identified; only 1 has a durable institutional commissioning page
how_to_read_it: Levy is directly confirmed by the hospital; Livingstone is reported by named MOH/hospital/CHAI technical personnel. No consolidated inventory or measured operational series is public.

latest_verified_actual: 7 new plants commissioned 2024–2026; duplex status not stated in any release
how_to_read_it: Public releases do not state whether new plants are duplex systems, so the target cannot be scored.

indicator: Public PSA plants (context inventory, not a stated target)
latest_verified_actual: Project and facility records identify at least 27 distinct facility sites with installed or commissioned PSA plants by Apr 2026 after reconciling known overlaps; current functionality is unknown
verdict: Substantial growth verified; no official current inventory
how_to_read_it: The circulating figure of 35 still appears in no primary source, but it is now plausible rather than disproven: verified project records cover 15 World Bank plants, 6 Global Fund–UNOPS plants, Kitwe, and independent/older sites. The defensible public minimum is 27 identifiable sites, not 35 functional plants.
